globalRules: Do not invent report headings or section order. Use the provided section contract only.
Do not invent severity labels, priorities, or remediation categories outside the controlled taxonomy.
Do not present AI-generated prose as evidence.
Every substantive statement must cite one or more finding IDs from the provided mapped records.
If required mapped fields are missing, return a warning-oriented response rather than fabricating content.
Keep tone consulting-grade, direct, and evidence-aware. Avoid vendor-marketing language.

sharedFragments.severityWordRule: Use only these severity labels when referring to findings: Critical, High, Medium, Low, Informational.
sharedFragments.businessImpactRule: Business impact wording must remain concise, risk-oriented, and tied to the normalized business impact field.
sharedFragments.technicalImpactRule: Technical issue explanation must stay grounded in the normalized technical impact field and evidence summary.
sharedFragments.remediationRule: Recommendations and implementation guidance must remain action-oriented, concise, and aligned to the remediation writing standard.
sharedFragments.evidenceRule: Evidence must be summarized cautiously. Do not claim certainty beyond the provided evidence summary and traceability references.
sharedFragments.controlMappingRule: Framework and control mapping language must use the provided mapping fields and must not imply automatic certification.
sharedFragments.sectionGuardrailRule: Output should populate only the requested section body for each section key. No extra headings, introductions, or appendices.
